metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_361
metadata.comment:
Rationale: In first attempts to dissect the molecular function of the putative transporter SLC10A4, the authors measured dopamine metabolite concentrations in nucleus accumbens and caudate putamen (both rich in dopaminergic neurons) (as shown in Fig. 3A) and concluded from the results that dopamine uptake and intracellular metabolism are affected. Additionally, dopamine metabolite ratios (table S2) indicated increased dopamine turnover in KO mice.
They showed significantly reduced dopamine uptake into SVs in slc10a4 KO mice (Fig. 4F), as compared to those derived from WT and slc10a4 overexpressing mice.
Chronoamperometric measurements in the striatum in vivo showed extrasynaptic accumulation of dopamine and reduced dopamine turnover in KO mice as compared to controls (Fig. 5).. Experimental description: SLC10A4 Null Mutant and tgNSE-SLC10A4 Mice (M&M)
The Slc10a4 null mutant (KO) mouse line was generated by Texas A&M Institute for
Genomic Medicine TIGM (TX, USA, http://www.tigm.org/).
